schemaChecks.evidence: All 22 required fields present: slug, title, category, parentTopic, layer, guideType, summary, quickAnswer, whenToUse, preferredAction, backupAction, stepByStepActions, warnings, whatNotToDo, redFlags, preparednessTips, sourceQuality, contentStatus, integrationDecision, upgradesGuide, appTags, sourceReferences. Plus images and constraint metadata fields.

whenToUse (4 items), stepByStepActions (0 items), warnings (7 items), whatNotToDo (6 items), redFlags (4 items), preparednessTips (6 items), appTags (9 items) — all flat arrays of strings.
All 6 sourceReferences entries have { title, organization, url, whyUseful } with non-null values.

constraintTags=['cant_move', 'alone'] and blockedByConstraints=[] — no overlap (blockedByConstraints is empty).
All field names use camelCase. No snake_case fields detected.
slug='trapping' is lowercase kebab-case with no spaces or uppercase.

safetyChecks.evidence: All claims in warnings, whatNotToDo, and redFlags are traceable to research packet findings: trap injury warnings (finding 18), cooking temperature 165F (finding 15), field dressing (finding 13), glove use (finding 14), diseased animals (finding 16), legal considerations (finding 17), scent control (finding 8). No unsourced safety claims.
All safety and guidance content maps to research packet findings from FM 21-76, USDA, Penn State Extension, USFWS, NIH, and RECOIL OFFGRID.
N/A — this is a reference_guide, not an action_card. stepByStepActions is correctly empty.
N/A — this is a reference_guide. Warnings are still provided (7 items) which is good practice.
N/A — this is a reference_guide. Red flags are still provided (4 items) which is good practice.

Evidence tier is authoritative_plus_field_practice. sourceQuality='mixed' is appropriate — core claims from FM 21-76 (authoritative) and USDA/Penn State (authoritative), but trap construction specifics rely on field-practice sources (RECOIL OFFGRID, Art of Manliness/BSA references).

Existing guide had 2 warnings: (1) 'Trapping and snaring in many areas is regulated or prohibited' — preserved and expanded in warnings[0]. (2) 'Improperly set traps can cause serious injury to animals, rescuers, or others' — preserved and expanded in warnings[1]. Both original redFlags also preserved: trap injury in redFlags[0], foodborne illness in redFlags[1].

duplicateChecks.evidence: upgradesGuide='trapping' — the slug 'trapping' exists in the guide library.

The upgrade corrects the parentTopic from 'Water Collection and Basic Treatment Without Equipment' to 'Emergency Food Procurement', which is a deliberate reclassification flagged in the planning packet. Category remains 'water_food'.

constraintChecks.evidence: responseRole='primary' in reference layer. No other reference-layer guide exists in the 'Emergency Food Procurement' parent topic. Sibling guides hold primary roles in action (primitive-fishing-survival), scenario (edible-plant-foraging-basics), and preparedness (emergency-food-procurement-prep) layers — no conflict.
alternativeToGuideSlugs=['primitive-fishing-survival', 'edible-plant-foraging-basics'] — both are in the same parentTopic 'Emergency Food Procurement'.

blockedByConstraints=[] — trapping knowledge is not blocked by any canonical constraint. Trapping is viable in most environments (deadfalls need no special equipment, snares need only wire/cordage). This is reasonable for a reference guide providing educational content.

warnings: sourceQuality is 'mixed' — some trap construction details rely on field-practice sources rather than government/institutional authorities. This is expected for the authoritative_plus_field_practice evidence tier and is honestly reflected.

The parentTopic is being reclassified from 'Water Collection and Basic Treatment Without Equipment' to 'Emergency Food Procurement'. This is a deliberate correction noted in the planning work item, not an error.

The 'notes' field in the guide JSON is not part of the NormalizedGuide schema — it should be removed before import. It is acceptable in pipeline artifacts but would need to be stripped at import time.
recommendation: Pass. All schema checks pass, no blocking issues found. The guide is a substantial upgrade from a stub (zero steps, zero sources, 1-sentence summary) to a well-sourced reference guide with 6 source references, 7 warnings, 4 red flags, 6 preparedness tips, and 2 image recommendations. The three warnings are informational — the mixed source quality is appropriate for the evidence tier, the parentTopic reclassification is intentional, and the notes field is a pipeline artifact that the import process handles. Ready for import/staging.
